title: S-Trap™ column digestion protocol (Protifi) of proteins for LC-MS / proteomics
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1eir6","text":"This protocol details the in-house BioMS procedure of S-Trap™ protein clean-up and subsequent column digestion/conversion of protein to peptide using trypsin.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f8trb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"931t1","text":"It is adapted from the long protocol from Protifi (as on August 2022) - https://files.protifi.com/protocols/s-trap-micro-long-4-7.pdf","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":72,"length":61}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"4A5F1C81221A11ED80D30A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://files.protifi.com/protocols/s-trap-micro-long-4-7.pdf"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"83h40","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fbgeh","text":"You may purchase S-trap columns and other consumables directly from us at the BioMS core facility on PPMS - https://corefacilities.manchester.ac.uk/?BioMS).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":156}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":108,"length":46}],"data":{}},{"key":"37sp4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a62vn","text":"Allow   for this process ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":6,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"afnma","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b2bus","text":"This protocol is to be used for protein loads columns are best for loadings less than   of starting material (protein). 1","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":119,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":120,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":86,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"bng1t","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dndka","text":"You have cell or tissue lysates in   or   of S-Trap lysis buffer (5% SDS with 50 mM TEAB pH 7.5). If your lysate volume is lower than these two volumes, make up to either with 5% S-Trap lysis buffer (working concentration). 2","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":224,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[{"key":3,"offset":35,"length":1},{"key":4,"offset":40,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"2isqb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"athu8","text":"SDS is essential for the S-trapping process and subsequent clean-up, it is important that samples contain close to   SDS. This can be achieved by dilution of the sample using the  stock of S-trap lysis buffer.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":5,"offset":115,"length":1},{"key":6,"offset":179,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"fehfh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e5nb6","text":"Protein lysates have been sheared of nucleic acid (e.g. from BioMS’s Covaris LE220+ AFA sonication system). Alternatively, a nuclease such as Benzonase may be used.3 ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":164,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6qra5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"19rkk","text":"Protein lysates have been reduced and alkylated 4  and clarified by centrifuging at   for   (see reduction and alkylation protocol in this series). ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":26,"length":7},{"style":"bold","offset":38,"length":10},{"style":"sup","offset":48,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":50,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[{"key":7,"offset":84,"length":1},{"key":8,"offset":90,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"2qtdf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6k4dh","text":"The protein concentration of a pool of the protein lysates has been quantified and samples prepared to be less than   of protein (Use the Millipore Direct Detect with the 5% SDS calibration file to check).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":9,"offset":116,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"7eh58","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"djetj","text":"Please take care not to touch the S-trap matrix in the column with pipette tips during the process.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dlmsc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eem5n","text":"Notes:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":6},{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5e7ob","text":"If you have more than   of protein - use the S-trap plate method \"S-Trap™ 96-well plate digestion protocol\" in this collection. ","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":128}],"entityRanges":[{"key":10,"offset":22,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"467n2","text":"Alternatively, if your lysate volume is larger than 50uL, simply remove a   sample aliquot for processing. ","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":107}],"entityRanges":[{"key":11,"offset":74,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"3lpv4","text":"This step is essential as omitting it may clog the S-Trap column. If you do not have access to ultrasonication, see protocol from EMDmillipore for details.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":155}],"entityRanges":[{"key":12,"offset":130,"length":12}],"data":{}},{"key":"248no","text":"If it is thought that the insoluble pellet contains proteins of interest, then proceed with homogenising (with a pipette and tip) the insoluble pellet as much as possible with fresh 5% S-trap lysis buffer. A suspension of insoluble particles may be formed this way. Proceed with a separate S-trap column to process this additional sample. steps: To the reduced and alkylated sample of volume either of 25 µL or 50 µL , add a volume of 2.5 µL or 5.0 µL respectively of 12 % (v/v) aqueous phosphoric acid at a ratio of 1:10 for a final concentration of 1.2 % (v/v) phosphoric acid and vortex mix. Total volume is now either 27.5 µL or 55 µL . This step is essential to completely denature proteins and trap them efficiently. The pH will be ≤ pH 1.0 . If the sample pH is not ≤ pH 1.0 , add additional phosphoric acid to reach pH ≤ pH 1.0 . A quick way to check the pH is to spot 2 µL of the acidified lysate on a strip of filter paper. The final phosphoric acid concentration is different between S-Trap micros, and minis/midis.

Add 165 µL or 330 µL of S-Trap binding buffer to the 27.5 µL or 55 µL volumes of acidified protein lysates respectively and mix. Total volume is now 192.5 µL for the 25 µL starting volume, or 385 µL for the 50 µL starting volumes.

Add enough of the acidified methanolic lysate to the S-Trap column. Do not add more sample than will fit the narrow “stem” of the S-Trap column. If the volume to be loaded is larger than will fit in the stem, then proceed to centrifugation (next step).

Centrifuge the column/tube combination at 4000 rcf, , for 00:02:00 in the Eppendorf 5430R centrifuge. Protein should be trapped within the protein-trapping matrix of the column. It is important not to let the liquid that passes through the S-Trap to come in contact with the protein-trapping matrix of the column.
Repeat the previous two steps if there is additional sample to be processed. Visually confirm all sample has passed through the column; if not, centrifuge again until all sample has passed through.

Wash captured protein with one wash of 150 µL of MTBE solution, simply add 150 µL of the MTBE solution to the column, and spin at 4000 x g, °C, for 00:02:00 . This will remove methanol-insoluble biomolecules from the quartz filter. For best results, rotate the S-Trap micro units (like a screw or knob) 180 degrees between the centrifugations of binding and wash steps. This is especially important when using a fixed-angle rotor because the spin column does not experience homogenous flow. A mark on the outside edge with a "Sharpie" type marker during centrifugation makes it easy to track rotations.

Following this, perform four washes of 150 µL of S-Trap binding buffer, again, add 150 µL of the S-trap binding buffer, and centrifuge at 4000 x g, °C, for 00:02:00 . Note: If you wish, you may transfer the flow through and washes back into an Eppendorf sample tube after each centrifugation step, otherwise empty the collection tube so that the washes do not come in contact with the binding matrix of the S-trap column. If discarding the washes then collect in a beaker and dispose in the acetonitrile/solvent waste when finished. Note: Depending on the number of protein samples you need to process, you may find that you need additional S-Trap binding buffer. If so, there are aliquots of 5 mL of 100 millimolar (mM) TEAB at pH 7.1 stored in 50 mL Falcon tubes in freezer 3. - take one out, thaw at Room temperature , and add 45 mL of methanol (located in fume hood) to make a final volume of 50 mL , mix, transfer to the bin.
Place the S-Trap column on top of a 2 mL Eppendorf tube. This will collect the flow-through.
Move S-Trap column to a clean digestion 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube.
Locate the trypsin aliquots. They are in the top shelf of freezer 3. Trypsin must be added to the protein at a ratio of 1:10 wt:wt (enzyme:protein).

The frozen aliquots are at a volume of 10 µL containing 20 µg of trypsin (concentration of 2 µg/µL ). 20 µL of digestion buffer is needed for each column. If working from 50 µg of protein starting material, then add 75 µL of digestion buffer to the trypsin aliquot. This gives a total volume of 85 µL , enough for 4 S-Trap digestions. If your amount of protein starting material is 25 µg , make up the aliquot with 155 µL of digestion buffer, enough for 8 S-Trap column digestions.

Add 20 µL of digestion buffer containing protease at 1:10 wt:wt into the top of the micro column. Use a gel loading tip (blue box) for this step. IMPORTANT! – Ensure there is no air bubble between the digestion buffer and the column matrix. The S-Trap binding matrix is hydrophilic and will absorb the digestion buffer when incubating. Correct loading of digestion buffer (no air bubble) incorrect loading of digestion buffer (air bubble present in column) Bubbles prevent the digestion buffer from entering the s-trap column matrix.
Cap the S-Trap column loosely to limit evaporative loss. A suggested way to do this is to close the cap until you feel resistance, then loosen one half-turn.
Incubate in the Eppendorf thermomixer for 01:00:00 at 47 °C for trypsin. Some dripping may occur during incubation; this is not of concern. REMEMBER - DO NOT SHAKE.
Add 65 µL of digestion buffer to the S-Trap column. Centrifuge the column / tube at 4000 x g, °C, for 00:02:00 , and collect.
Add 65 µL of 0.1% aqueous formic acid (FA) to the S-Trap column.
Centrifuge the column / tube at 4000 x g, °C, for 00:02:00 , and collect, this is now combined with the first elution through the centrifugation process.
Add 30 µL of 30% aqueous acetonitrile containing 0.1% formic acid. Centrifuge the column / tube at 4000 x g, °C, for 00:02:00 , and collect.

This elution assists in recovery of hydrophobic peptides. This is now combined with the the first and second elutions. The total volume will be approximately 200 µL . The final acetonitrile concentration will be around 5 % (v/v) .
Proceed to R3 desalting or store in a refrigerator. When you are ready, please dispose of any solvent waste in the non-chlorinated waste drum.
If you wish, you may also set up this digestion step overnight, with no impact on the S-trap process. To do this, set the Thermomixer to 37 °C and incubate overnight, again with no shaking.
creator: ronan o'cualain
